title: cleome kalachariensis subsp. namibensis
intro: Cleome kalachariensis subsp. namibensis, also known as the 'Kalahari rocket', is a perennial plant that belongs to the Cleome genus and is native to the savannas and grasslands of southern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cleome kalachariensis subsp. namibensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, or it can be applied topically as a poultice or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
